source_selection.adjudication_basis: Selected heading on line 640 using normalized heading matching and narrative scoring; considered 1 viable background-like candidate(s).

notes: This grouped actor refers to the four unnamed parties that expressed interest in late March 2016 before later named Interested Parties were assigned Company 1 through Company 4 labels.

actors.group_label: eleven industry participants contacted by financial advisors
notes: This contact-count group includes Pfizer and the four late-March companies, so it overlaps with named bidders and should not be used as a distinct participant set for deduped bidder totals.

actors.display_name: Unnamed other parties with confidentiality agreements
notes: The filing does not state the count in this sentence; later text identifies the Interested Parties as Pfizer and Company 1 through Company 4, with Sanofi also under confidentiality agreement but outside the Interested Parties label.

spans.anchor_text: Company 4 submitted a preliminary proposal
spans.quote_text: metrics. Company 4 submitted a preliminary proposal to acquire Medivation for $63.00 per Share in cash, plus CVRs of up to $5.00 per Share conditioned on certain regulatory milestones for pipeline assets.

spans.anchor_text: Company 4 declined to submit a definitive proposal
spans.quote_text: ceased participation in the strategic transaction process. The definitive proposals, in order of increasing valuation, were from Company 3 for $72.50 per Share in cash, Company 1 for $73.00 per Share in cash, Company 2 for $75.50 per Share in cash, and Pfizer for $77.00 per Share in cash. Together with their definitive proposals, each of Company 1 and Company 3 also submitted a revised version of the merger agreement.

spans.anchor_text: determined to engage Evercore Group L. L. C.

spans.anchor_text: expressed Pfizer’s continued interest in participating

events.summary: Four other parties indicated interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
Sanofi initiated contact on an unsolicited basis to discuss a possible strategic transaction with Medivation.
Medivation had J. P. Morgan retained as independent financial advisor to Medivation.
notes: Block states J. P. Morgan was retained in connection with routine advanced preparedness for defense and general strategic advice.

events.summary: Sanofi expressed interest in pursuing a possible strategic transaction but did not indicate a price.
Sanofi submitted a non-binding proposal to acquire Medivation for $52.50 per share.

terms.raw_text: for $52.50 per share of Medivation’s Common Stock

events.summary: Pfizer indicated interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
The Medivation board determined to engage Evercore as an additional independent financial advisor to Medivation.
Sanofi publicly announced its $52.50 per share proposal by press release.
Pfizer expressed continued interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.

date.raw_text: Early on the morning of May 5, 2016

events.summary: Sanofi issued a press release restating the terms of its $52.50 proposal.
Pfizer reiterated its interest in participating in any negotiated transaction process that Medivation might initiate.
The Medivation board decided to control a process to explore transaction value and directed its advisors to contact select industry participants.
notes: Board determination followed consideration of Sanofi’s ongoing consent solicitation and the possibility of an increased but still unattractive public offer.

events.summary: Sanofi submitted an increased proposal of $58.00 per share in cash plus a contingent value right of up to $3.00 per share.
notes: Additional contingent value right of up to $3.00 per share conditioned on certain revenue metrics is described in the text.

terms.raw_text: $58.00 per share in cash plus a contingent value right ("CVR") of up to $3.00 per share

events.summary: Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with Pfizer.
Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sanofi, under which Sanofi agreed to terminate its consent solicitation and accept a six-month standstill.
Sanofi agreed to terminate its consent solicitation in connection with entering into a confidentiality agreement with Medivation.
notes: Termination described as part of the confidentiality agreement terms.

events.summary: Pfizer expressed continued interest in exploring a potential negotiated strategic transaction with Medivation.
J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent process letters to each Interested Party requesting written non-binding preliminary proposals for a potential acquisition of Medivation by August 8, 2016.

deadline_date.raw_text: by 5:00 p. m. Pacific Time on August 8, 2016

events.summary: Interested Parties submitted non-binding written preliminary proposals for the acquisition of Medivation.
Company 1 submitted a preliminary all-cash proposal to acquire Medivation for $71.00 per share.

terms.raw_text: all-cash acquisition of Medivation for $71.00 per Share

events.summary: Company 2 submitted a preliminary all-cash proposal to acquire Medivation for $62.00 to $64.00 per share.

terms.raw_text: all-cash acquisition of Medivation for $62.00—$64.00 per Share

events.summary: Company 3 submitted a preliminary proposal to acquire Medivation for $60.00 per share in cash plus CVRs of up to $10.00 per share conditioned on certain revenue metrics.
notes: Proposal included CVRs of up to $10.00 per share conditioned on certain revenue metrics.

terms.raw_text: $60.00 per Share in cash, plus CVRs of up to $10.00 per Share

events.summary: Company 4 submitted a preliminary proposal to acquire Medivation for $63.00 per share in cash plus CVRs of up to $5.00 per share conditioned on certain regulatory milestones for pipeline assets.
notes: Proposal included CVRs of up to $5.00 per share conditioned on certain regulatory milestones for pipeline assets.

terms.raw_text: $63.00 per Share in cash, plus CVRs of up to $5.00 per Share

events.summary: Pfizer submitted a preliminary all-cash proposal to acquire Medivation for $65.00 per share.

terms.raw_text: all-cash acquisition of Medivation for $65.00 per Share

events.summary: The Medivation board decided that Company 2 and Company 3 would not be invited to continue in the strategic transaction process because their preliminary proposals were not competitive with those of the other Interested Parties.
events.drop_reason_text: their preliminary proposals were not competitive with those of the other Interested Parties

events.summary: Company 2 said by telephone that it would submit a revised initial all-cash bid proposal for Medivation at $70.00 per share.
notes: The filing states Company 2 communicated by telephone that it would submit a revised initial bid proposal.

terms.raw_text: an all-cash acquisition of Medivation at $70.00 per Share

events.summary: Company 2 submitted a written revised all-cash proposal for the acquisition of Medivation at $70.00 per share.

terms.raw_text: all-cash acquisition of Medivation, at $70.00 per Share

events.summary: Company 3 submitted a written revised all-cash proposal for the acquisition of Medivation at $70.50 per share.

terms.raw_text: all-cash acquisition of Medivation, at $70.50 per Share

events.summary: J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent instruction letters to each Interested Party requiring definitive written proposals by noon Pacific Time on August 19, 2016.

deadline_date.raw_text: by 12:00 p. m. Pacific Time on Friday, August 19, 2016

events.summary: Company 3 notified Medivation's financial advisors that it would not be submitting a revised proposal.
events.drop_reason_text: Company 3 notified Medivation’s financial advisors that it would not be submitting a revised proposal.

events.summary: Company 4 declined to submit a definitive proposal and ceased participation in the strategic transaction process.
events.drop_reason_text: Company 4 declined to submit a definitive proposal.

events.summary: Pfizer, Company 1, Company 2 and Company 3 submitted definitive proposals for an all-cash acquisition of Medivation.
Company 1 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal for Medivation at $73.00 per share.
Company 2 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal for Medivation at $75.50 per share.
Company 3 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal for Medivation at $72.50 per share.
Pfizer submitted a definitive all-cash proposal for Medivation at $77.00 per share.
The Medivation board authorized advisors to tell the remaining Interested Parties that best and final proposals were due by noon Pacific Time on August 20, 2016.

deadline_date.raw_text: on August 20, 2016, by 12:00 p. m. Pacific Time

events.summary: Following approval of the Medivation board, Pfizer and Medivation executed the merger agreement.
Pfizer and Medivation executed the merger agreement.
notes: Cross-filing evidence used because the supplied chronology blocks do not contain the execution sentence.

date.raw_text: Later that morning, on August 20, 2016, beginning at 11:51 a. m. Pacific Time

events.summary: Pfizer, Company 1 and Company 2 submitted final proposals, each approved by their respective boards, not subject to further diligence, and accompanied by revised draft merger agreements.

date.raw_text: Later that morning, on August 20, 2016, beginning at 11:51 a. m. Pacific Time

events.summary: Company 1 submitted a revised final proposal to acquire Medivation for $80.25 per share.

date.raw_text: Later that morning, on August 20, 2016, beginning at 11:51 a. m. Pacific Time

events.summary: Company 2 submitted a revised final proposal to acquire Medivation for $80.00 per share.

date.raw_text: Later that morning, on August 20, 2016, beginning at 11:51 a. m. Pacific Time

events.summary: Pfizer submitted a revised final proposal to acquire Medivation for $81.50 per share.
The parties issued a joint press release announcing the transaction.

exclusions.explanation: General background that the board frequently reviewed strategic alternatives and from time to time received inquiries; no discrete dated event extracted.
Board discussions, rejections, diligence access, management presentations, and process administration are not event types in the allowed taxonomy for this extraction call.
The July 5 press release restated Sanofi’s previously received $58.00 plus CVR proposal and the board’s prior rejection; proposal itself already captured from June 27 block.
Press releases or filings about board rejection, earnings call, HSR filing, consent solicitation, and consent revocation are outside the permitted event taxonomy used here.

Advisor outreach to multiple industry participants reflects process activity but not a separately classified event type under the allowed taxonomy absent specific participant-level expressed interest or proposals.

Confidentiality agreements with unnamed additional parties are not emitted individually because the counterparties cannot be confidently linked from the text of those blocks to specific actor_ids without inventing or guessing mappings.

These blocks are diligence, board deliberation, fairness-opinion, rationale, or agreement-review context rather than standalone taxonomy events, or they duplicate events captured from adjacent blocks.
Company 4's failure to submit a definitive proposal and cessation of participation is one drop event described across two adjacent blocks.
The supplied chronology blocks in this chunk are fairness-opinion discussion, risk factors, and projection disclosures, not merger-process events under the allowed taxonomy.

unresolved_mentions: The 'fourth industry participant' in B055 and 'fifth industry participant' in B056 appear to complete the set later labeled Company 1-4 plus Pfizer, but the filing does not identify which Company number corresponds to each execution date.
The 'one additional party' in B047 is unnamed at that point and could be one of Company 1-4, but the text does not specify which one.

The 'three industry participants' and 'a fourth industry participant' in B006 may correspond to the later four late-March companies, but the filing does not map each early mention to Company 1-4 individually.
The count assertion of 15 parties contacted in B090 exceeds the 12 industry participants described in B045; the additional parties are not individually identified in the supplied text.
The grouped actor 'eleven industry participants' overlaps with Pfizer and the four late-March companies; it should not be treated as a unique non-overlapping bidder set.
a fifth industry participant initially contacted by Medivation’s financial advisors
a fourth industry participant initially contacted by Medivation’s financial advisors
one additional party initially contacted by Medivation’s financial advisors
extraction_notes: Coverage limited to supplied chronology blocks through B059 in chunk-1.
No final-round, drop, or execution events are available within these chronology blocks.
Whole-company scope is marked unknown where the text only refers to strategic discussions or a negotiated transaction process without explicit whole-company proposal terms.
For the June 27 Sanofi proposal, the per-share cash amount is captured in value_per_share and the CVR component is preserved in notes/raw_text rather than converted into a single per-share figure.
Chunk coverage begins at July 15, 2016 and captures late-stage process events through signing and public announcement.
No NDA events were emitted in this chunk because no executed confidentiality agreements are described in the supplied chronology blocks.
No proposal event was emitted for August 18 marked-up merger agreement submissions because the text provides no explicit economics for those submissions.
Consideration type for the August 20 final proposals is left as unknown because the specific sentence states only per-share prices, though surrounding context suggests cash.
Chunk-3 contains no new chronology events in the supplied blocks themselves.
One execution event was supported using the cross-filing appendix.
No proposal, NDA, round, or drop events are extractable from B108-B165 because those blocks do not narrate dated process steps.
